commands.repair_reason: Human revision and owner/security review are needed because this external command-execution PR is proof-gated and has concrete security-sensitive blockers.; Needs attention: The command-execution hardening diff introduces availability and sensitive-data-disclosure concerns.; Review finding: [P1] Allow Windows path separators in cmd validation — src/process/exec.ts:45 - [P2] Permit equals signs in wrapper arguments —... (sha=dc1c169fada3a006b251353fea64aae217b7b456)
commands.review_summary:
The branch expands Windows cmd.exe argument validation in `src/process/exec.ts`, adds PowerShell/null/control-character checks, and exports a validation helper.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR regression by source inspection: on `win32`, `.cmd` and `.bat` commands flow through `buildCmdExeCommandLine(resolvedCommand, ...)`, and the PR validates that resolved path plus normal args with a blocklist that rejects path separators and common flags. I did not run live Windows proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human revision and owner/security review are needed because this external command-execution PR is proof-gated and has concrete security-sensitive blockers.
